summary: Integrated workflow judgment into claim-domain decision output and MCP decision response while preserving route_decisions, review_items, action_packet_candidates, source_refs, pii_profile, non_execution_semantics, and the 25-tool local MCP order. Reopen addendum fixed the real domain/MCP boundary so eight semireal workflow scenarios match expected stage, action, posture, and remediation loop from structured support surfaces.

reopen_addendum.blocker: Global review found that the real evaluate_claim_domain_decision and MCP decision surface collapsed eight semireal workflow scenarios to evidence_completion because structured legal/evidence/finance support surfaces were not consumed at the public decision boundary.
reopen_addendum.fix: domain_workflow_integration now consumes payload-provided evidence_checkpoint, finance_bridge, and legal_checkpoints surfaces before falling back to deterministic defaults. It accepts those generic surfaces either at the adapter payload top level or nested under workflow_support. The MCP handler forwards the same result unchanged. The semireal fixture helper forwards structured workflow_support objects into those standard payload surfaces.

risks: Local python lacks pytest, so pytest commands were run through uv with explicit pytest and pydantic dependencies.

If all structured support is stripped, deterministic fallback remains conservative and advisory-only; exact semireal scenario differentiation is preserved when support is either projected into standard evidence_checkpoint, finance_bridge, and legal_checkpoints payload fields or retained under workflow_support.
